place: Whitesboro CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Whitesboro was founded around 1901 by the Equitable Industrial Association, a Black‑owned investment group that included poet Paul Laurence Dunbar.
The town was named Whitesboro after its most famous investor, George H., a former North Carolina congressman.
By 1909, Whitesboro boasted two churches, an industrial school, a railroad station, a post office, and a hotel—all built by residents.
The Whitesboro Historical Foundation, founded by Shirley Green, opened a museum to showcase the town’s unique Black‑founding heritage.
Every year the Whitesboro Historic Preservation Project hosts a Black History Gala at the Cape May Convention Hall, drawing community pride.
In 2023 the Foundation held a community listening session at the historic Whitesboro Grammar School to shape future revitalization plans.
